content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4-xhigh

## Summary

Found 2 correctness regressions: the patch makes `project create-from-bead` fail on transient `br list` outages, including prompt-override flows, and the new shared-acceptance expansion can misclassify upstream dependencies as planned-elsewhere work.

## Amendments

1. `project create-from-bead` now hard-fails on any non-corrupt `br list --json` failure because `handle_create_from_bead` unconditionally does `load_bead_summaries(&current_dir).await?` at `src/cli/project.rs:321`, and `tests/cli.rs:2024` was flipped to lock in that behavior. That is a user-visible regression outside prompt rendering: before this change the command could still build a project from `br show` plus the milestone plan, and the override path still does not need generated prompt context at all because `create_project_from_bead_context` bypasses rendering when `prompt_override` is present (`src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs:400`). Please restore the old best-effort fallback for transient `br list` I/O failures, and reserve hard failure for genuinely corrupt bead data.
   Rationale: This bead was supposed to extend prompt generation while preserving the `9ni.6.1` contract and stable behavior elsewhere. The current change turns a recoverable metadata lookup failure into a full command failure, including `--prompt-file` flows that should not depend on prompt generation.
2. The new shared-acceptance-owner expansion in `build_planned_elsewhere_context` (`src/cli/project.rs:1294` and `src/cli/project.rs:1336`) does not exclude direct upstream dependencies. If an acceptance criterion is jointly owned by the active bead and one of its blocking dependencies, that blocker will now be rendered again in `Already Planned Elsewhere` as `shared milestone acceptance ownership ...`, even though dependencies already have their own dedicated section in the prompt (`src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs:982`). That duplicates the same bead under two different semantics and re-labels prerequisite work as out-of-scope/planned-elsewhere work. Filter `upstream_ids` out of the shared-ownership pass, or merge the extra acceptance-ownership detail into the existing dependency entry instead of emitting a second planned-elsewhere item.
   Rationale: The prompt contract separates dependency context from planned-elsewhere scope boundaries. Mixing blockers into the latter section can mislead downstream agents about what is prerequisite work versus work they must explicitly not absorb.
